response.justification: Bogdan brings a genuinely strong distributed systems background — notably the Dunnhumby microservices work handling millions of HTTP requests per second — and has held Staff-level titles at multiple organizations with evidence of technical breadth and craftsmanship. The re:cinq role shows recent, direct engagement with agentic infrastructure and MCP tooling, which aligns well with the AI-native engineering environment. However, Python is listed as a must-have and is entirely absent from the resume; the primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. The AI/agentic experience is also shallow at roughly six months, and the current caregiving gap introduces timeline uncertainty. The profile is compelling enough to warrant a screen call specifically to probe the Python gap and depth of agentic work.
strengths: Hands-on high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O architecture
Staff Engineer title and technical strategy ownership across multiple roles with demonstrated breadth across stacks, platforms, and domains
Direct recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume and skills list — it is a stated must-have, and no proxy experience (e.g., data pipelines, ML tooling) is mentioned
AI and agentic experience is limited to a single six-month engagement; the role treats AI-native development as a baseline expectation, not a differentiator
Current career gap since October 2025 with no stated return timeline introduces uncertainty around availability and recency of hands-on technical work
